The Aerial Bold project is a crowdsourcing tool that allows users to find and catalogue letters, numbers, and characters in satellite images. The project was set up by German designer Benedikt Groß and geographer Joey Lee, who raised $11,492 through a Kickstarter campaign. The tool uses Mapbox images and allows users to scan through a grid of 21 rectangles, suggesting letters, numbers, or characters in each rectangle. Users can then place selection boxes over the relevant parts of the map and add the letters to a database. The NHS has spent nearly £350,000 on removing tattoos for people who changed their minds about their body art. Over 2,000 people have received free laser treatment in the past four years. The NHS only provides tattoo removal if it is necessary for health reasons, such as significant distress or mental health problems.
